# browsegrab

> Token-efficient browser agent for local LLMs — Playwright + accessibility tree + MarkGrab, MCP native.

## Purpose

browsegrab provides programmatic browser automation optimized for low-token-budget LLM agents. It renders pages via Playwright and exposes the accessibility tree (~500-1,500 tokens per step) instead of raw HTML, with optional MarkGrab integration for clean markdown extraction. Elements are referenced by short IDs (e1, e2, ...) for compact action commands.

The core architecture uses a dual-path DOM compression strategy:
- **Path A (Structure)**: Accessibility tree via Playwright's `locator.aria_snapshot()` — interactive elements get short ref IDs for LLM action targeting.
- **Path B (Content)**: MarkGrab markdown extraction (on-demand) — clean text for reading/summarization tasks.

This combination achieves ~500-1,300 tokens per step versus 4K-10K tokens for raw HTML or screenshot-based alternatives.

## Capabilities

- **Accessibility tree snapshots**: Navigate to a URL and get a token-efficient tree representation of the page with ref IDs for all interactive elements
- **Element interaction**: Click, type, scroll, go back, wait — all using short ref IDs (e1, e2, ...) from the accessibility tree
- **Content extraction**: Extract page content as compressed DOM (AX tree + markdown via MarkGrab)
- **Agentic browsing**: LLM-driven multi-step browsing toward an objective with action planning, loop detection, history compression, and success pattern caching
- **MCP server**: 8 browser automation tools exposed via FastMCP (stdio transport)
- **4-strategy selector resolution**: ref -> ARIA role -> CSS -> text fallback chain for robust element targeting
- **5-stage JSON parser**: Robust extraction of action JSON from LLM responses (direct, code block, brace boundary, key-value cleanup, regex)
- **Loop detection**: Sliding-window pattern detection prevents infinite agent loops
- **Success pattern cache**: Domain-based file cache stores successful action sequences for workflow replay
- **3 LLM providers**: vLLM, Ollama, OpenAI-compatible — all via httpx, no SDK dependencies

## Installation

```
pip install browsegrab          # core (Playwright + httpx)
pip install browsegrab[cli]     # CLI (click + rich)
pip install browsegrab[mcp]     # MCP server (fastmcp)
pip install browsegrab[content] # content extraction (markgrab)
pip install browsegrab[all]     # everything
```

After installing, install Playwright browsers:
```
playwright install chromium
```

**Requirements**: Python >= 3.11, playwright >= 1.49, httpx >= 0.28

**Optional dependencies**:
- `markgrab >= 0.1.2` — content extraction (markdown conversion)
- `fastmcp >= 2.0` — MCP server
- `click >= 8.0` + `rich >= 13.0` — CLI interface

## Quick Use

### CLI

```bash
browsegrab snapshot https://example.com                          # accessibility tree snapshot
browsegrab snapshot https://example.com -f json                  # JSON output
browsegrab snapshot https://example.com -i                       # interactive elements only
browsegrab extract https://example.com                           # extract content as markdown
browsegrab extract https://example.com -s "main"                 # scoped extraction
browsegrab extract https://example.com -j                        # JSON output
browsegrab browse https://example.com "find the pricing page"    # agentic browse
browsegrab browse https://example.com "find pricing" -n 5        # max 5 steps
browsegrab browse https://example.com "find pricing" -j          # JSON output
```

### Python

```python
from browsegrab import BrowseGrabConfig, BrowseSession

async with BrowseSession(BrowseGrabConfig.from_env()) as session:
    await session.navigate("https://example.com")
    snap = await session.snapshot()          # SnapshotResult
    content = await session.extract_content() # markdown string
    result = await session.browse("https://example.com", "find pricing")  # BrowseResult
```

### MCP

Server: `browsegrab-mcp` (stdio transport)
Tools: `browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_type`, `browser_snapshot`, `browser_scroll`, `browser_extract_content`, `browser_go_back`, `browser_wait`

## API Reference

### Configuration

#### `BrowseGrabConfig`

Top-level configuration aggregating all subsystem configs. All configs can be loaded from environment variables via `from_env()`.

```python
@dataclass
class BrowseGrabConfig:
    browser: BrowserConfig = field(default_factory=BrowserConfig)
    llm: LLMConfig = field(default_factory=LLMConfig)
    snapshot: SnapshotConfig = field(default_factory=SnapshotConfig)
    agent: AgentConfig = field(default_factory=AgentConfig)

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> BrowseGrabConfig
```

#### `BrowserConfig`

Playwright browser configuration.

```python
@dataclass
class BrowserConfig:
    headless: bool = True                   # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_HEADLESS
    timeout_ms: int = 30_000                # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_TIMEOUT_MS
    viewport_width: int = 1280              # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH
    viewport_height: int = 720              # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT
    user_agent: str | None = None           # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_USER_AGENT
    locale: str = "en-US"                   # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_LOCALE
    ignore_https_errors: bool = False       # BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> BrowserConfig
```

#### `LLMConfig`

LLM provider configuration.

```python
@dataclass
class LLMConfig:
    provider: Literal["vllm", "ollama", "openai"] = "vllm"  # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_PROVIDER
    base_url: str = "http://localhost:8000/v1"               # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_BASE_URL
    model: str = "Qwen/Qwen3.5-32B-AWQ"                     # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MODEL
    api_key: str | None = None                               # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_API_KEY
    timeout_s: int = 60                                      # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_TIMEOUT_S
    max_retries: int = 2                                     # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MAX_RETRIES
    temperature: float = 0.0                                 # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_TEMPERATURE
    max_tokens: int = 1024                                   # BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MAX_TOKENS

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> LLMConfig
```

#### `SnapshotConfig`

Snapshot and DOM compression configuration.

```python
@dataclass
class SnapshotConfig:
    max_snapshot_length: int = 5000         # BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_MAX_LENGTH
    max_content_length: int = 3000          # BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH
    include_links: bool = True              # BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_INCLUDE_LINKS
    filter_interactive_only: bool = False   # BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_INTERACTIVE_ONLY

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> SnapshotConfig
```

#### `AgentConfig`

Agent loop configuration.

```python
@dataclass
class AgentConfig:
    max_steps: int = 10                     # BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_MAX_STEPS
    history_max_entries: int = 5            # BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_HISTORY_MAX
    cache_dir: str = "~/.cache/browsegrab"  # BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_CACHE_DIR
    enable_cache: bool = True               # BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_ENABLE_CACHE
    loop_detection_window: int = 3          # BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_LOOP_WINDOW

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> AgentConfig
```

### BrowseSession

Main orchestrator: URL -> browser actions -> LLM planning -> results. Supports async context manager.

```python
class BrowseSession:
    def __init__(
        self,
        config: BrowseGrabConfig | None = None,
        manager: BrowserManager | None = None,
    ) -> None
```

**Parameters**:
- `config` (`BrowseGrabConfig | None`): Configuration. Defaults to `BrowseGrabConfig()` with all default values.
- `manager` (`BrowserManager | None`): Existing browser manager to reuse. If None, creates and owns a new one.

#### `BrowseSession.navigate`

```python
async def navigate(self, url: str, **expectation: Any) -> ActionResult
```

Navigate to a URL.

- `url` (`str`): Target URL.
- `**expectation`: Optional keyword args passed to post-action handler (e.g. `include_snapshot=True`).
- **Returns**: `ActionResult` with success status, URL, title, optional snapshot.

#### `BrowseSession.snapshot`

```python
async def snapshot(self) -> SnapshotResult
```

Take an accessibility tree snapshot of the current page. Clears and rebuilds the internal RefMap.

- **Returns**: `SnapshotResult` with tree text, elements list, ref count, URL, title, token estimate.

#### `BrowseSession.click`

```python
async def click(self, ref: str, **expectation: Any) -> ActionResult
```

Click an element by ref ID.

- `ref` (`str`): Element ref ID from snapshot (e.g. "e1", "e5").
- `**expectation`: Optional post-action options.
- **Returns**: `ActionResult`.

#### `BrowseSession.type`

```python
async def type(
    self,
    ref: str,
    text: str,
    clear: bool = True,
    submit: bool = False,
    **expectation: Any,
) -> ActionResult
```

Type text into an element.

- `ref` (`str`): Element ref ID for the input field.
- `text` (`str`): Text to type.
- `clear` (`bool`): Clear existing content before typing. Default `True`.
- `submit` (`bool`): Press Enter after typing. Default `False`.
- `**expectation`: Optional post-action options.
- **Returns**: `ActionResult`.

#### `BrowseSession.scroll`

```python
async def scroll(
    self,
    direction: str = "down",
    amount: int = 500,
    ref: str | None = None,
    **expectation: Any,
) -> ActionResult
```

Scroll the page or a specific element.

- `direction` (`str`): One of "up", "down", "left", "right". Default "down".
- `amount` (`int`): Scroll amount in pixels. Default 500.
- `ref` (`str | None`): Optional element ref to scroll into view first.
- `**expectation`: Optional post-action options.
- **Returns**: `ActionResult`.

#### `BrowseSession.go_back`

```python
async def go_back(self, **expectation: Any) -> ActionResult
```

Navigate back in browser history.

- `**expectation`: Optional post-action options.
- **Returns**: `ActionResult`.

#### `BrowseSession.wait`

```python
async def wait(
    self,
    ms: int = 1000,
    selector: str | None = None,
    state: str = "visible",
) -> ActionResult
```

Wait for time or element state.

- `ms` (`int`): Milliseconds to wait (or timeout for selector). Default 1000.
- `selector` (`str | None`): CSS selector to wait for.
- `state` (`str`): Target element state: "visible", "hidden", "attached", "detached". Default "visible".
- **Returns**: `ActionResult`.

#### `BrowseSession.extract_content`

```python
async def extract_content(
    self,
    max_length: int | None = None,
    scope: str | None = None,
) -> str
```

Extract page content as compressed DOM (AX tree + optional MarkGrab markdown).

- `max_length` (`int | None`): Override max content length.
- `scope` (`str | None`): CSS selector to limit extraction scope (e.g. "main", ".article").
- **Returns**: `str` — compressed DOM text with page info header and content.

#### `BrowseSession.browse`

```python
async def browse(
    self,
    url: str,
    objective: str,
    max_steps: int | None = None,
) -> BrowseResult
```

Agentic browsing: LLM plans and executes actions to achieve an objective.

- `url` (`str`): Starting URL.
- `objective` (`str`): What the agent should accomplish.
- `max_steps` (`int | None`): Override max steps (default from `config.agent.max_steps`, which is 10).
- **Returns**: `BrowseResult` with extracted content, action history, step count, token usage, timing.

#### `BrowseSession.close`

```python
async def close(self) -> None
```

Close the session, browser context, and optionally the browser manager (if owned by this session).

### Result Types

#### `SnapshotResult`

```python
@dataclass
class SnapshotResult:
    tree_text: str                              # formatted AX tree string with [ref=eN] annotations
    elements: list[RefElement] = []             # all interactive elements found
    ref_count: int = 0                          # number of interactive elements
    url: str = ""                               # current page URL
    title: str = ""                             # current page title
    token_estimate: int = 0                     # estimated token count (~len/4)

    def get_element(self, ref: str) -> RefElement | None
        # Look up element by ref ID (e.g. "e3")
```

#### `RefElement`

```python
@dataclass
class RefElement:
    ref: str            # e.g. "e1", "e2"
    role: str           # e.g. "link", "button", "textbox"
    name: str           # accessible name
    tag: str = ""       # HTML tag name
    value: str = ""     # current value (for inputs)
    level: int = 0      # heading level
    focused: bool = False
    checked: bool | None = None    # for checkboxes
    expanded: bool | None = None   # for accordions
    selector: str = ""             # resolved CSS selector
```

#### `ActionResult`

```python
@dataclass
class ActionResult:
    success: bool                       # whether the action succeeded
    action: str                         # "click", "navigate", "type", "scroll", "go_back", "wait", "extract"
    target: str = ""                    # ref ID or URL
    url: str = ""                       # current page URL after action
    title: str = ""                     # current page title after action
    snapshot: str | None = None         # AX tree text if requested via expectation
    content: str | None = None          # markdown content if requested
    error: str | None = None            # error message on failure
    token_estimate: int = 0             # estimated tokens consumed

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]
        # Convert to dict, omitting None values
```

#### `StepRecord`

```python
@dataclass
class StepRecord:
    step: int           # step number (0-based: 0 = initial navigate)
    action: str         # action name
    target: str = ""    # ref ID or URL
    url: str = ""       # page URL after action
    summary: str = ""   # brief summary or error
    success: bool = True
```

#### `BrowseResult`

```python
@dataclass
class BrowseResult:
    success: bool                           # whether the objective was achieved
    url: str = ""                           # final page URL
    title: str = ""                         # final page title
    content: str = ""                       # extracted markdown content
    snapshot: str = ""                      # final AX tree snapshot
    steps: list[StepRecord] = []            # full action history
    total_steps: int = 0                    # total steps taken
    total_tokens: int = 0                   # estimated total tokens consumed
    processing_time_ms: float = 0.0         # wall-clock time in milliseconds
    error: str | None = None                # error message if failed
    metadata: dict[str, Any] = {}           # extensible metadata

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]
        # Convert to serializable dict (steps serialized as list of dicts)
```

### Browser Management

#### `BrowserManager`

Manages Playwright browser lifecycle with async context manager support.

```python
class BrowserManager:
    def __init__(self, config: BrowserConfig | None = None) -> None

    async def new_context(self, **overrides: object) -> BrowserContext
        # Create a new browser context with configured defaults.
        # overrides are passed to playwright browser.new_context().

    async def new_page(self, **context_overrides: object) -> Page
        # Create a new page in a fresh context.

    async def close(self) -> None
        # Close browser and Playwright.

    @property
    def is_running(self) -> bool
        # Check if browser is active and connected.
```

**Module-level functions**:

```python
async def get_manager(config: BrowserConfig | None = None) -> BrowserManager
    # Get or create the default BrowserManager singleton.

async def close_manager() -> None
    # Close the default BrowserManager singleton.
```

### DOM Compression

#### `compress_dom`

```python
async def compress_dom(
    page: Page,
    ref_map: RefMap,
    config: SnapshotConfig | None = None,
    include_content: bool = False,
    scope_selector: str | None = None,
) -> str
```

Produce a compressed DOM representation for LLM consumption. Combines Path A (accessibility tree) and Path B (MarkGrab markdown, on-demand).

- `page` (`Page`): Playwright page.
- `ref_map` (`RefMap`): RefMap to populate (cleared and rebuilt).
- `config` (`SnapshotConfig | None`): Snapshot configuration.
- `include_content` (`bool`): Whether to include MarkGrab content section. Default `False`.
- `scope_selector` (`str | None`): CSS selector to limit content extraction scope.
- **Returns**: `str` — text with `[Page: ...]`, `[URL: ...]`, `[Elements: N interactive]` header, AX tree, and optional `--- Content ---` section.

### Ref System

#### `RefMap`

Bidirectional mapping between ref IDs and accessibility tree elements. Ref IDs follow the pattern `e1`, `e2`, `e3`, ...

```python
@dataclass
class RefMap:
    def clear(self) -> None
        # Reset the ref map for a new snapshot.

    def assign(self, role: str, name: str, **attrs: Any) -> RefElement
        # Assign a new ref ID to an element.
        # attrs: tag, value, level, focused, checked, expanded, selector

    def get(self, ref: str) -> RefElement | None
        # Look up element by ref ID.

    def all_elements(self) -> list[RefElement]
        # Return all mapped elements in assignment order.

    @property
    def count(self) -> int
        # Number of elements in the map.

    def is_interactive(self, role: str) -> bool
    def is_landmark(self, role: str) -> bool
    def should_include(self, role: str) -> bool
```

**Interactive roles**: link, button, textbox, checkbox, radio, combobox, listbox, option, menuitem, tab, switch, slider, spinbutton, searchbox, menuitemcheckbox, menuitemradio, treeitem

**Landmark roles**: heading, navigation, main, banner, contentinfo, complementary, form, region, dialog, alertdialog, alert

### Selector Resolution

#### `resolve_ref`

```python
async def resolve_ref(page: Page, ref: str, ref_map: RefMap) -> Locator | None
```

Resolve a ref ID to a Playwright Locator using 4-strategy fallback.

Strategy order (fastest first):
1. **Role selector** (ARIA role + name) — ~5ms
2. **CSS selector** (if stored in RefElement) — ~1ms
3. **Text selector** (accessible name) — ~10ms
4. **None** (all strategies failed)

- `page` (`Page`): Current Playwright page.
- `ref` (`str`): Ref ID (e.g. "e1", "e5").
- `ref_map` (`RefMap`): Current RefMap with element info.
- **Returns**: `Locator | None`.

### Snapshot

#### `take_snapshot`

```python
async def take_snapshot(
    page: Page,
    ref_map: RefMap,
    config: SnapshotConfig | None = None,
) -> SnapshotResult
```

Take an accessibility tree snapshot of the page. Uses Playwright's `locator.aria_snapshot()` for compact YAML-like output, then augments interactive elements with `[ref=eN]` annotations.

- `page` (`Page`): Playwright page to snapshot.
- `ref_map` (`RefMap`): RefMap instance (will be cleared and rebuilt).
- `config` (`SnapshotConfig | None`): Snapshot configuration.
- **Returns**: `SnapshotResult` with formatted tree text and ref elements.

### LLM Providers

#### `LLMProvider` (abstract base)

```python
class LLMProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def chat(
        self,
        messages: list[dict[str, str]],
        temperature: float = 0.0,
        max_tokens: int = 1024,
    ) -> str
        # Send chat completion, return response text.

    @abstractmethod
    async def is_available(self) -> bool
        # Check if provider endpoint is reachable.

    @property
    @abstractmethod
    def name(self) -> str
        # Provider name ("vllm", "ollama", "openai").

    @property
    @abstractmethod
    def model(self) -> str
        # Model name being used.

    async def plan_action(
        self,
        system_prompt: str,
        context: str,
        history: str,
        objective: str,
    ) -> dict[str, Any]
        # Convenience method: build messages, call chat, parse JSON response.
        # Returns parsed action dict (e.g. {"action": "click", "ref": "e5"}).
```

#### `VLLMProvider`

```python
class VLLMProvider(LLMProvider):
    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str = "http://localhost:8000/v1",
        model: str = "Qwen/Qwen3.5-32B-AWQ",
        api_key: str | None = None,
        timeout_s: int = 60,
    ) -> None
```

vLLM OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Sends `chat_template_kwargs: {"enable_thinking": false}` to disable reasoning parser.

#### `OllamaProvider`

```python
class OllamaProvider(LLMProvider):
    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434",
        model: str = "qwen2.5:14b",
        timeout_s: int = 60,
    ) -> None
```

Ollama API provider. Uses `/api/chat` endpoint with `stream: false`.

#### `OpenAICompatProvider`

```python
class OpenAICompatProvider(LLMProvider):
    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str = "http://localhost:8000/v1",
        model: str = "gpt-4",
        api_key: str | None = None,
        timeout_s: int = 60,
    ) -> None
```

Generic OpenAI-compatible endpoint provider.

#### `get_provider`

```python
def get_provider(config: LLMConfig | None = None) -> LLMProvider
```

Factory: create an LLM provider from config. Dispatches based on `config.provider` value ("vllm", "ollama", "openai").

### JSON Parser

#### `parse_action_json`

```python
def parse_action_json(text: str) -> dict[str, Any]
```

Parse LLM response into an action dict using 5-stage fallback.

**Stages**:
1. Direct JSON parse
2. Code block extraction (`` ```json ... ``` ``)
3. Brace boundary detection (outermost `{ ... }`)
4. Key-value cleanup (fix quotes, trailing commas, unquoted keys) + retry
5. Regex extraction of action/ref/text/url/direction/submit/result/reason/ms fields

- `text` (`str`): Raw LLM response text.
- **Returns**: `dict[str, Any]` with at least an `"action"` key.
- **Raises**: `ValueError` if all 5 stages fail.

**Valid actions**: `click`, `type`, `scroll`, `navigate`, `go_back`, `wait`, `extract`, `done`, `fail`

### Prompt System

#### `build_action_prompt`

```python
def build_action_prompt(
    objective: str,
    snapshot: str,
    history: str = "",
    cached_hint: str = "",
) -> list[dict[str, str]]
```

Build the message list for LLM action planning.

- `objective` (`str`): User's goal.
- `snapshot` (`str`): Current page AX tree snapshot.
- `history` (`str`): Compressed action history.
- `cached_hint` (`str`): Optional hint from success pattern cache.
- **Returns**: List of message dicts (`[{"role": "system", ...}, {"role": "user", ...}]`).

The system prompt (~400 tokens) instructs the LLM to respond with JSON-only actions using ref IDs.

### Agent Intelligence

#### `PatternCache`

```python
class PatternCache:
    def __init__(self, cache_dir: str = "~/.cache/browsegrab") -> None

    def lookup(self, url: str, objective: str) -> list[dict] | None
        # Look up cached action sequence for domain + objective.
        # Returns list of action dicts or None.

    def store(self, url: str, objective: str, actions: list[dict]) -> None
        # Store a successful action sequence. Max 20 entries per domain.

    def get_hint(self, url: str, objective: str) -> str
        # Get compact hint string for LLM context (up to 5 steps).
        # Returns empty string if no cache hit.

    def clear(self, domain: str | None = None) -> None
        # Clear cache for a specific domain or all.
```

Cache location: `~/.cache/browsegrab/patterns.json`. Domain-based lookup with exact objective matching (case-insensitive).

#### `compress_history`

```python
def compress_history(steps: list[StepRecord], max_entries: int = 5) -> str
```

Compress action history into a compact string for LLM context (~100-300 tokens).

Strategy: keep first entry + summarize repeated patterns in middle + keep last N entries.

- `steps` (`list[StepRecord]`): Full step history.
- `max_entries` (`int`): Maximum entries to include. Default 5.
- **Returns**: `str` — formatted history text.

#### `LoopGuard`

```python
@dataclass
class LoopGuard:
    window_size: int = 3          # number of recent actions to check
    max_repeats: int = 2          # repetitions before flagging as loop

    def record(self, action: str, target: str = "") -> None
        # Record an action for loop detection.

    def is_looping(self) -> bool
        # Check if recent actions form a repeating pattern.
        # Detects: (1) same action N times in a row, (2) pattern repetition (A-B-A-B).

    def get_escape_hint(self) -> str
        # Generate hint text for LLM to break out of the loop.

    def reset(self) -> None
        # Reset loop guard state.
```

### MCP Server Tools

The MCP server exposes 8 tools via FastMCP (stdio transport). Start with `browsegrab-mcp`.

#### `browser_navigate`

```python
async def browser_navigate(url: str, wait_until: str = "domcontentloaded") -> str
```

Navigate to a URL and return accessibility tree snapshot.

- `url` (`str`): The URL to navigate to.
- `wait_until` (`str`): When to consider navigation done ("domcontentloaded", "load", "networkidle").
- **Returns**: JSON string with keys: `success`, `url`, `title`, `snapshot`, `ref_count`, `token_estimate`, `error`.

#### `browser_click`

```python
async def browser_click(ref: str) -> str
```

Click an element by its ref ID.

- `ref` (`str`): Element ref ID from the accessibility tree snapshot (e.g. "e1", "e5").
- **Returns**: JSON string (ActionResult.to_dict()).

#### `browser_type`

```python
async def browser_type(ref: str, text: str, clear: bool = True, submit: bool = False) -> str
```

Type text into an input element.

- `ref` (`str`): Element ref ID (e.g. "e3" for a textbox).
- `text` (`str`): The text to type.
- `clear` (`bool`): Clear existing content before typing. Default `True`.
- `submit` (`bool`): Press Enter after typing. Default `False`.
- **Returns**: JSON string (ActionResult.to_dict()).

#### `browser_snapshot`

```python
async def browser_snapshot(interactive_only: bool = False, max_length: int = 5000) -> str
```

Get the current page accessibility tree snapshot.

- `interactive_only` (`bool`): Only show interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs). Default `False`.
- `max_length` (`int`): Maximum snapshot text length. Default 5000.
- **Returns**: JSON string with keys: `url`, `title`, `ref_count`, `token_estimate`, `snapshot`.

#### `browser_scroll`

```python
async def browser_scroll(direction: str = "down", amount: int = 500, ref: str | None = None) -> str
```

Scroll the page or a specific element.

- `direction` (`str`): Scroll direction ("up", "down", "left", "right"). Default "down".
- `amount` (`int`): Scroll amount in pixels. Default 500.
- `ref` (`str | None`): Optional element ref ID to scroll into view first.
- **Returns**: JSON string (ActionResult.to_dict()).

#### `browser_extract_content`

```python
async def browser_extract_content(scope: str | None = None, max_length: int = 3000) -> str
```

Extract page content as clean markdown (via MarkGrab if available).

- `scope` (`str | None`): CSS selector to limit extraction scope (e.g. "main", ".article").
- `max_length` (`int`): Maximum content length. Default 3000.
- **Returns**: JSON string with key: `content`.

#### `browser_go_back`

```python
async def browser_go_back() -> str
```

Navigate back in browser history.

- **Returns**: JSON string (ActionResult.to_dict()).

#### `browser_wait`

```python
async def browser_wait(ms: int = 1000, selector: str | None = None, state: str = "visible") -> str
```

Wait for a specified time or until an element reaches a state.

- `ms` (`int`): Milliseconds to wait (or timeout for selector wait). Default 1000.
- `selector` (`str | None`): CSS selector to wait for.
- `state` (`str`): Target element state ("visible", "hidden", "attached", "detached"). Default "visible".
- **Returns**: JSON string (ActionResult.to_dict()).

### CLI Commands

Entry point: `browsegrab` (requires `pip install browsegrab[cli]`)

#### `browsegrab snapshot <url>`

Take an accessibility tree snapshot of a URL.

- `--format, -f` (`tree|text|json`): Output format. Default "tree".
- `--interactive-only, -i`: Show only interactive elements.

#### `browsegrab extract <url>`

Extract page content as compressed DOM.

- `--scope, -s` (`str`): CSS selector to scope content extraction.
- `--json-output, -j`: Output as JSON.

#### `browsegrab browse <url> <objective>`

Agentic browse: LLM plans actions to achieve an objective.

- `--steps, -n` (`int`): Max steps. Default 10 (from config).
- `--json-output, -j`: Output as JSON.

## Examples

### Basic Snapshot

```python
import asyncio
from browsegrab import BrowseGrabConfig, BrowseSession

async def main():
    config = BrowseGrabConfig()
    async with BrowseSession(config) as session:
        await session.navigate("https://news.ycombinator.com")
        snap = await session.snapshot()

        print(f"Page: {snap.title}")
        print(f"URL: {snap.url}")
        print(f"Interactive elements: {snap.ref_count}")
        print(f"Token estimate: {snap.token_estimate}")
        print(snap.tree_text[:500])

        # Look up a specific element
        el = snap.get_element("e1")
        if el:
            print(f"e1: {el.role} '{el.name}'")

asyncio.run(main())
```

### Manual Step-by-Step Browsing

```python
import asyncio
from browsegrab import BrowseGrabConfig, BrowseSession

async def main():
    config = BrowseGrabConfig.from_env()
    async with BrowseSession(config) as session:
        # Navigate
        result = await session.navigate("https://github.com")
        print(f"Navigated: {result.url}")

        # Take snapshot to see available elements
        snap = await session.snapshot()
        print(snap.tree_text[:1000])

        # Type into search box (find the right ref from snapshot)
        await session.type("e3", "browsegrab", submit=True)

        # Wait for results
        await session.wait(ms=2000)

        # Take another snapshot
        snap = await session.snapshot()
        print(snap.tree_text[:1000])

        # Click a result
        await session.click("e5")

        # Extract content
        content = await session.extract_content()
        print(content[:500])

asyncio.run(main())
```

### Agentic Browsing with Custom Config

```python
import asyncio
from browsegrab import BrowseGrabConfig, BrowseSession
from browsegrab.config import LLMConfig, AgentConfig

async def main():
    config = BrowseGrabConfig(
        llm=LLMConfig(
            provider="ollama",
            base_url="http://localhost:11434",
            model="qwen2.5:14b",
            temperature=0.0,
            max_tokens=512,
        ),
        agent=AgentConfig(
            max_steps=15,
            history_max_entries=3,
            loop_detection_window=4,
        ),
    )

    async with BrowseSession(config) as session:
        result = await session.browse(
            url="https://docs.python.org",
            objective="Find the documentation for asyncio.run",
            max_steps=10,
        )

        print(f"Success: {result.success}")
        print(f"Steps taken: {result.total_steps}")
        print(f"Tokens used: {result.total_tokens}")
        print(f"Time: {result.processing_time_ms:.0f}ms")

        if result.error:
            print(f"Error: {result.error}")

        if result.content:
            print("--- Content ---")
            print(result.content[:1000])

        # Inspect step history
        for step in result.steps:
            status = "OK" if step.success else "FAIL"
            print(f"  [{status}] Step {step.step}: {step.action} -> {step.target}")

asyncio.run(main())
```

### Scoped Content Extraction

```python
import asyncio
from browsegrab import BrowseGrabConfig, BrowseSession

async def main():
    async with BrowseSession(BrowseGrabConfig()) as session:
        await session.navigate("https://example.com/blog/article")

        # Extract only the main article content
        content = await session.extract_content(scope="article.post-content")
        print(content)

        # Extract just the sidebar
        sidebar = await session.extract_content(scope="aside.sidebar")
        print(sidebar)

asyncio.run(main())
```

### Using BrowserManager Directly

```python
import asyncio
from browsegrab.browser.manager import BrowserManager
from browsegrab.browser.snapshot import take_snapshot
from browsegrab.config import BrowserConfig, SnapshotConfig
from browsegrab.dom.ref_map import RefMap

async def main():
    manager = BrowserManager(BrowserConfig(headless=True, viewport_width=1920, viewport_height=1080))

    async with manager:
        page = await manager.new_page()
        await page.goto("https://example.com")

        ref_map = RefMap()
        snap = await take_snapshot(page, ref_map, SnapshotConfig(filter_interactive_only=True))
        print(f"Found {snap.ref_count} interactive elements")

        for el in snap.elements:
            print(f"  {el.ref}: [{el.role}] {el.name}")

asyncio.run(main())
```

### Using the Pattern Cache

```python
from browsegrab.agent.cache import PatternCache

cache = PatternCache("~/.cache/browsegrab")

# Store a successful workflow
cache.store(
    url="https://example.com",
    objective="find the pricing page",
    actions=[
        {"action": "click", "ref": "e3"},
        {"action": "click", "ref": "e7"},
        {"action": "done", "result": "Found pricing at /pricing"},
    ],
)

# Later: look up cached workflow
actions = cache.lookup("https://example.com/other-page", "find the pricing page")
if actions:
    print("Cache hit:", actions)

# Get LLM hint
hint = cache.get_hint("https://example.com", "find the pricing page")
print(hint)
# Output:
# Previously successful steps:
# 1. click -> e3
# 2. click -> e7
# 3. done -> Found pricing at /pricing

# Clear cache
cache.clear(domain="example.com")
```

### Custom LLM Provider

```python
from browsegrab.llm.base import LLMProvider

class MyProvider(LLMProvider):
    @property
    def name(self) -> str:
        return "custom"

    @property
    def model(self) -> str:
        return "my-model"

    async def chat(self, messages, temperature=0.0, max_tokens=1024) -> str:
        # Your LLM call here
        return '{"action": "done", "result": "completed"}'

    async def is_available(self) -> bool:
        return True
```

### MCP Server Integration

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "browsegrab": {
      "command": "browsegrab-mcp",
      "env": {
        "BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_HEADLESS": "true",
        "BROWSEGRAB_LLM_PROVIDER": "ollama",
        "BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MODEL": "qwen2.5:14b"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Loop Detection

```python
from browsegrab.agent.loop_guard import LoopGuard

guard = LoopGuard(window_size=3, max_repeats=2)

guard.record("click", "e5")
guard.record("click", "e5")
guard.record("click", "e5")

print(guard.is_looping())       # True
print(guard.get_escape_hint())  # WARNING: You are stuck in a loop ...

guard.reset()
```

## Configuration

All configuration is driven by environment variables or dataclass defaults. Every environment variable uses the `BROWSEGRAB_` prefix.

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_HEADLESS` | `true` | Run browser headless |
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_TIMEOUT_MS` | `30000` | Default page timeout (ms) |
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH` | `1280` | Browser viewport width |
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT` | `720` | Browser viewport height |
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_USER_AGENT` | (none) | Custom user agent string |
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_LOCALE` | `en-US` | Browser locale |
| `BROWSEGRAB_BROWSER_IGNORE_HTTPS_ERRORS` | `false` | Ignore HTTPS certificate errors |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_PROVIDER` | `vllm` | LLM provider: vllm, ollama, openai |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_BASE_URL` | `http://localhost:8000/v1` | LLM endpoint base URL |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MODEL` | `Qwen/Qwen3.5-32B-AWQ` | Model name |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_API_KEY` | (none) | API key for authenticated endpoints |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_TIMEOUT_S` | `60` | LLM request timeout (seconds) |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MAX_RETRIES` | `2` | Max retries on LLM failure |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_TEMPERATURE` | `0.0` | Sampling temperature |
| `BROWSEGRAB_LLM_MAX_TOKENS` | `1024` | Max tokens to generate |
| `BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_MAX_LENGTH` | `5000` | Max AX tree snapshot length (chars) |
| `BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH` | `3000` | Max content extraction length (chars) |
| `BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_INCLUDE_LINKS` | `true` | Include links in snapshot |
| `BROWSEGRAB_SNAPSHOT_INTERACTIVE_ONLY` | `false` | Show only interactive elements |
| `BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_MAX_STEPS` | `10` | Max agent steps per browse |
| `BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_HISTORY_MAX` | `5` | Max history entries for LLM context |
| `BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_CACHE_DIR` | `~/.cache/browsegrab` | Pattern cache directory |
| `BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_ENABLE_CACHE` | `true` | Enable success pattern caching |
| `BROWSEGRAB_AGENT_LOOP_WINDOW` | `3` | Loop detection window size |

### LLM Action JSON Schema

The LLM is instructed to respond with one of these JSON actions per step:

```
{"action": "click", "ref": "eN"}
{"action": "type", "ref": "eN", "text": "...", "submit": true|false}
{"action": "scroll", "direction": "down|up|left|right", "amount": 500}
{"action": "navigate", "url": "..."}
{"action": "go_back"}
{"action": "wait", "ms": 1000}
{"action": "extract"}
{"action": "done", "result": "..."}
{"action": "fail", "reason": "..."}
```
